trialDescriptions.title: Untersuchung eines positiven Versorgungseffektes der NichtraucherHelden-App (Medizin): Durchführung eines Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT)
trialDescriptions.summary: Die NichtraucherHelden-App dient der Behandlung und Linderung einer diagnostizierten Tabakabhängigkeit. Durch ein kognitiv-verhaltenstherapeutisches Nichtrauchercoaching wird Patientinnen und Patienten geholfen, ihre Tabakabhängigkeit zu überwinden. Ziel dieser Studie ist, die Wirksamkeit der App beim Rauchstopp zusammen mit einem ärztlichen Kurzgespräch zu untersuchen.
trialDescriptions.scientificSummary:
Das Ziel dieser Studie ist die wissenschaftliche Evaluation der NichtraucherHelden-App (Medizin) in Kombination mit einer ärztlichen Kurzberatung (brief advice). Es erfolgt die Prüfung der Wirksamkeit der NichtraucherHelden-App (Medizin) in Kombination mit einem brief advice (Interventionsgruppe, IG) im Vergleich zu einer Kontrollgruppe (KG), die ausschließlich einen brief advice erhält. Folgende Parameter werden im Rahmen der Studie erhoben:
• 7-Tage-Punktprävalenz der Rauchabstinenz 6 Monate nach dem Rauchstopp 
• Prolongierte Rauchabstinenz
• Objektive Rauchabstinenz mittels Cotinin-Nachweis im Speichel
• Veränderungen der gesundheitsbezogenen Lebensqualität
• Einfluss auf die Luftnot
• Einfluss auf den Husten

trialDescriptions.title: Investigation of a positive care effect of the NichtraucherHelden App (Medicine): Implementation of a Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT).
trialDescriptions.summary: The NichtraucherHelden App is used to treat and alleviate a diagnosed tobacco addiction. Through a cognitive-behavioral therapy approach, patients are encouraged to overcome their tobacco dependence. The aim of this study is to investigate the effectiveness of the app in smoking cessation together with a brief medical consultation.
trialDescriptions.scientificSummary:
The aim of this study is the scientific evaluation of the NichtraucherHelden App (Medicine) in combination with brief advice from a physician. The effectiveness of the NichtraucherHelden App (Medicine) in combination with brief advice (intervention group, IG) is tested in comparison to a control group (KG), which only receives brief advice. The following parameters will be evaluated as part of the study:
• 7-day point prevalence of smoking abstinence 6 months after smoking cessation. 
• prolonged smoking abstinence
• objective smoking abstinence measured by cotinine detection in saliva
• changes in health-related quality of life
• influence on shortness of breath
• influence on cough

observationalGroups.comment: Interventionsgruppe (IG). Probanden erhalten eine 3-minütige, ärztliche Kurzberatung und die NichtraucherHelden-App (Medizin). Am Ende der Studie erfolgt bei erfolgreichem Rauchstopp eine Cotinin-Messung im Speichel.

Intervention Group (IG). Subjects receive a 3-minute, brief medical consultation and the NichtraucherHelden App (Medicine). At the end of the study, successful non-smokers provide a saliva sample for cotinine measurement.
Kontrollgruppe (KG). Probanden erhalten eine 3-minütige, ärztliche Kurzberatung, jedoch keine App. Sie unternehmen den Rauchstopp selbstständig.
Control group (CG). Subjects receive a 3-minute, brief medical consultation, but no app.

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome: 7-Tage-Punktprävalenz der Rauchabstinenz 6 Monate nach dem Rauchstopp
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome:
- Prolongierte Rauchabstinenz
- Objektive Rauchabstinenz mittels Cotinin-Nachweis im Speichel
- Veränderungen der gesundheitsbezogenen Lebensqualität
- Einfluss auf die Luftnot
- Einfluss auf den Husten

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome: 7-day point prevalence of smoking abstinence 6 months after smoking cessation
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome:
- prolonged smoking abstinence
- objective smoking abstinence measured by cotinine detection in saliva
- changes in health-related quality of life
- influence on shortness of breath
- influence on cough

trialResultsDescriptions.briefSummaryOfResultsDescription:
Background
Smoking tobacco implies significant health hazards. Digital cessation support can get more smokers in contact with guideline-based cessation. The objective was to test the efficacy of a guideline-based smoking cessation app (NichtraucherHelden®). The hypothesis was a significant higher cessation rate in the intervention group.

Methods
The study was a nationwide, multicentric, prospective, parallel, randomized controlled trial in Germany from November 2021 to March 2023. Recruitment took place in medical practices and by telephone via study centres. Eligible participants were adult tobacco-dependent smokers according to ICD-10 (F17.2). Randomization (1:1) was operated by a computer-generated stratified 1:1 block procedure. Intervention (IG; n = 336) and control group (CG; n = 325) were briefly advised with regard to stop smoking, IG was additionally treated with the cessation app. Primary endpoint was the self-reported 7-day-point-abstinence after 6 months with an intention to treat analysis. Secondary endpoints comprised prolonged abstinence and biochemically verified abstinence. The study was registered at the German Registry of Clinical Trials (DRKS00025933, UTN U1111-1268-2181) and was approved by the competent ethic committees (leading ethic committee Berlin #Eth-52/20).

Results
336 participants (IG) and 325 (CG) were analysed. Seven-day point prevalence was significantly higher in the app group (IG) (20% vs. 10%, OR 2.2 (1.4 – 3.4)). Additionally, the prolonged abstinence and the objective abstinence rate were significantly higher in the app group.

Conclusions
The NichtraucherHelden App doubles the abstinence rate. Apps can bridge the gap between the small numbers of therapeutic offers and the need for modern evidence-based cessation support.

Implications
The study is the first to provide evidence for the feasibility and efficacy of guideline-based digital smoking cessation provided by a smartphone app for the German statutory health insurance system (SHI). Smoking cessation support by smartphone apps could be broadly distributed and thus bring more smokers in contact with guideline-based cessation support than to date and increase the number of successful quitters substantially.

publications.description: Rupp A, Rietzler S, Di Lellis MA, Weiland T, Tschirner C, Kreuter M. Digital smoking cessation with a comprehensive guideline-based app - results of a nationwide, multicentric, parallel, randomized controlled trial in Germany. Nicotine Tob Res. 2024 Jan 18:ntae009. doi: 10.1093/ntr/ntae009. Epub ahead of print. PMID: 38243574.

studiedHealthConditionDescriptions.comment: Mental and behavioural disorders due to use of tobacco
ICD-10 F17.2: Psychische und Verhaltensstörungen durch Tabak; Abhängigkeitssyndrom
ICD-10 F17.2:  Mental and behavioural disorders due to use of cannabinoids; Dependence syndrome

institutes.city: mehrere Allgemeinarzt- + Pneumologie-Praxen an verschiedenen Standorten

recruitmentDescriptions.exclusionCriteria:
- Begleitende Alkohol-, Tabletten- oder illegale Drogensucht
- Akut-symptomatische psychiatrische Erkrankung
- Schwere kognitive Einschränkungen
- Demenzielle Erkrankungen
- Mangelnde deutsche Sprachkenntnisse
- Arbeit in 24h-Schichten (z.B. Rettungsdienst, Feuerwehr)
- Teilnahme an einem anderen Programm zur Tabakentwöhnung
- Nutzung einer anderen Smartphone App zur Tabakentwöhnung
recruitmentDescriptions.inclusionCriteria:
- Diagnostizierte Tabakabhängigkeit nach ICD-10 F17.2
- Absicht, mit dem Rauchen aufzuhören 
- Alter ≥ 18 Jahre 
- Endgerät zur Nutzung der NichtraucherHelden-App (Medizin) 
- Wohnhaft (Erstwohnsitz) in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland 
- Valide private E-Mail-Adresse

recruitmentDescriptions.exclusionCriteria:
- concomitant alcohol, pill, or illicit drug addiction
- acutely symptomatic psychiatric illness
- severe cognitive impairment
- dementia-related illnesses
- lack of German language skills
- work in 24h shifts (e.g. rescue service, fire department)
- participation in another tobacco cessation program
- use of another smartphone app for tobacco cessation
recruitmentDescriptions.inclusionCriteria:
- diagnosed tobacco dependence according to ICD-10 F17.2
- intention to quit smoking 
- age ≥ 18 years 
- device for use of the NichtraucherHelden App (Medicine) 
- resident (primary residence) in Germany 
- valid private e-mail address
